parsed.query: Exa official MCP web search API content highlights autoprompt neural keyword search agent context docs benchmark
parsed.executedQuery: Exa official MCP web search API content highlights autoprompt neural keyword search agent context docs benchmark (site:docs.exa.ai OR site:exa.ai)

constraints.focus: remote MCP endpoint, default and optional tools, search result context shape, highlights, text contents, neural search, autoprompt, benchmarks for agent search

results.title: Exa | Web Search API, AI Search Engine, & Website Crawler
results.description: The best search API for AI · Highest performance across every use case · Powerful features built into your search · Deep web research with structured outputs.

chunks.text: [Exa Highlights: Quality, Token-Efficient Search](https://exa.ai/blog/highlights-for-agents) [Exa Highlights: Quality, Token-Efficient Search](https://exa.ai/blog/highlights-for-agents) [Read more](https://exa.ai/blog/highlights-for-agents)
> StackAI and Exa are the dream team…Exa is amazing at web search! We're proud to use Exa on our platform and to show our customers how web search can power AI agent workflows.

results.title: How to Evaluate Exa Search
results.description: Comprehensive guide to benchmarking Exa's search API: methodology, optimal settings, datasets, and quality-latency tradeoffs.

chunks.text:
- [Overview](https://exa.ai/docs/reference/evaluating-exa-search#overview)
- [Best Practice: Start with Defaults](https://exa.ai/docs/reference/evaluating-exa-search#best-practice-start-with-defaults)
- [Compare Within Latency Classes](https://exa.ai/docs/reference/evaluating-exa-search#compare-within-latency-classes)
- [Search Types: Understanding the Quality-Latency Spectrum](https://exa.ai/docs/reference/evaluating-exa-search#search-types-understanding-the-quality-latency-spectrum)
- [Fast Search](https://exa.ai/docs/reference/evaluating-exa-search#fast-search)
- [Auto Search (Default)](https://exa.ai/docs/reference/evaluating-exa-search#auto-search-default)
- [Deep Search](https://exa.ai/docs/reference/evaluating-exa-search#deep-search)
- [Neural Search](https://exa.ai/docs/reference/evaluating-exa-search#neural-search)
- [Evaluating Exa with Tool Calling](https://exa.ai/docs/reference/evaluating-exa-search#evaluating-exa-with-tool-calling)
- [Why Tool Calling Matters for Evaluatio

```
# Option 1: Use text with character limit (recommended for consistent comparisons)
exa.search_and_contents(
    query,
    type="auto",  # or "fast" (for `Deep`, see Option 2)
    num_results=10,
    text={"max_characters": 15000}
)

# Option 3 (Deprecated): Use context string for RAG
# Note: The `context` parameter is deprecated. Use `text` or `highlights` instead.
# exa.search_and_contents(
#     query,
#     type="deep",
#     additional_queries=["variation 1", "variation 2"],
#     num_results=10,
#     context={"max_characters": 20000}  # Deprecated
# )

results.title: llms-full.txt - Exa
results.description: We're launching a big update to Auto search in our API. The new system cannot create useful scores for results. Because of this, we're removing scores from Auto ...

chunks.text: Exa's search endpoint previously charged separately for search results and their webpage contents. Now, contents for 10 search results per request are included for free, and as a result API usage is cheaper for the majority of users.

\`\`\`

\

 Claim: The Eiffel Tower is an iconic iron lattice structure\
 Assessment: supported\
 Confidence Score: 1\

 \- https://www.toureiffel.paris/en/news/130-years/what-eiffel-tower-made...\
 \- https://thechalkface.net/resources/melting\_the\_eiffel\_tower.pdf...\
 \- https://datagenetics.com/blog/april22016/index.html...\
 \- https://engineering.purdue.edu/MSE/aboutus/gotmaterials/Buildings/patel.html...\
 \- https://www.toureiffel.paris/en/news/130-years/how-long-can-tower-last...\
 Refuting Sources:\
\
```
